policy_statement: P-3AC (NAP) defines result indicators and targets for 2020 and 2030 to monitor adaptation progress.
mechanism_description: The programme defines result indicators and targets to track adaptation progress, supporting planning and management of climate adaptation actions.
primary_causal_pathway: Monitoring + indicators → improved adaptive management/planning → ↓ vulnerability/↓ exposure → ↓ climate risk impacts
causal_mechanism_detail:
1) Changed activity/system: Public-sector adaptation governance—setting result indicators, targets, and monitoring systems for adaptation actions and outcomes.  
2) Mediating process: Measurement, reporting, and feedback loops enable adaptive management (course-correction, resource allocation, prioritization, and performance accountability) and improve the quality and timeliness of adaptation planning decisions.  
3) Climate-relevant outcome: More effective implementation of adaptation measures, resulting in reduced vulnerability/exposure and lower damages/health impacts from climate hazards (e.g., heat, floods, drought).
dominant_pathway_test: The single biggest climate-relevant effect is improved decision-making and accountability that leads to better-designed and better-timed measures that reduce vulnerability and climate hazard impacts.
mechanism_classification_reasoning:
1) Dominant causal pathway: The mechanism operates through monitoring and indicators that strengthen planning and adaptive management, ultimately reducing vulnerability/exposure to climate hazards.  
2) Why this primary over alternatives: While improved monitoring can indirectly influence mitigation co-benefits (e.g., tracking energy resilience), the core and largest effect of this mechanism is enabling adaptation effectiveness rather than directly changing emissions, resource throughput, or ecosystem carbon.  
3) Edge cases applied: If indicators were explicitly for GHG inventories/targets, the category could be Mitigation; here they are explicitly “adaptation progress” indicators, so Adaptation is correct.  
4) Applies to ALL instances: Any policy whose mechanism is “adaptation monitoring indicators → adaptation planning support” has the same primary purpose and effect—better adaptation governance leading to reduced climate risk impacts.  
5) Secondary subtype deferred: Any finer subtype (e.g., heat-risk, flood-risk, health resilience, critical infrastructure resilience) depends on what the monitored adaptation actions address and must be assigned at the individual policy level.
mechanism_edge_case_notes: If the indicator framework is primarily used to comply with emissions reporting/mitigation targets or to optimize resource efficiency (rather than adaptation outcomes), the dominant pathway could shift; otherwise, classification as Adaptation is straightforward.
key_indicators: “Result indicators”; “targets”; “defined under the NAP”; “monitor adaptation progress”; “2020 and 2030”
co_benefits: Improved public-sector performance management and transparency (general governance benefits beyond climate adaptation)
instance_edge_case_notes: Could also be interpreted as an information/M&E framework (information-disclosure) if the indicators are publicly reported; however, the text foregrounds defining indicators and targets, so target-setting is the best fit.
